_comment: Your structured career facts. The builder drafts a tailored resume + cover letter FROM this — it never invents anything not here. Copy to apply_engine/builder/profile.json (git-ignored) and replace with your own. Every bullet should be a true, specific fact; the LLM selects and rephrases them to fit each job, it does not fabricate.
headline: Mechanical / Simulation Engineer — AI-native automation
summary_facts: Engineer who pairs deep simulation/optimization expertise with AI-native software delivery.
Ships production automation by directing LLM coding harnesses; owns architecture through operation.
Generalist strength: connecting physics, optimization, data, and product.

bullets: Built and rolled out 5 internal automation tools (test pipeline, root-cause agent, multi-objective optimizer, report generation, enablement frameworks), cutting routine analysis from days to minutes.
Led finite-element simulation (LS-DYNA, HyperWorks) and design-of-experiments optimization for new product development.
Correlated simulation against physical test data to validate models and de-risk design decisions before tooling.
Championed AI-orchestrated engineering across the R&D team; authored enablement docs adopted by peers.
Developed FEA workflows (ANSYS) for load-bearing components and automated post-processing of result files.
Reduced a manual stress-report process to an automated pipeline, saving ~50% of analysis time.

education.detail: Thesis on surface texturing of load-bearing polymer components; FEA-to-experiment correlation.

goals: Forward-deployed / applied-AI or product-engineering roles where domain depth meets shipping real AI systems.
interests: Agentic tooling, engineering simulation + AI, and turning manual analysis into automated systems.
personal_thread: Lifelong competitor; engineering is how I compete now. I learn by shipping the thing I need before anyone assigns it.
